rationale: The citing paper uses Zhao, Hoeffler, and Zauberman (2007) to support a key theoretical link: higher versus lower construal shifts preference toward desirability versus feasibility attributes. This citation is not just a passing list item; it is invoked in the conceptual development and again when motivating Study 4's desirability-feasibility product-choice predictions. Although the 2015 paper introduces a different antecedent (perceived height), it relies on the cited paper as prior empirical grounding for the construal-to-preference relationship.
relation_description: Aggarwal and Zhao treat Zhao et al. (2007) as prior evidence that construal level changes desirability-versus-feasibility weighting in choice. They build on that foundation by proposing physical height as a new driver of construal and testing downstream preference effects in similar trade-off choices.
